items.name: MOLLE 4K Rucksack System (OCP)
items.description: CIF-issued MOLLE 4K rucksack, OCP pattern, with nametape and cat-eyes on top flap
items.whyNeeded: The primary load-bearing system for all field operations. CIF issue is mandatory — no substitute.
items.selectionTips: Ensure nametape and cat-eye tabs are sewn on the top flap before arrival. Frame NSN: 8465-01-673-3400.

recommendedProducts.name: ALICE Large Field Pack with Frame
recommendedProducts.reason: While the MOLLE 4K is mandatory CIF issue, an aftermarket ruck may be authorized at cadre discretion. The ALICE frame remains the proven standard for SFQC ruck weights.

items.name: Fighting Load Carrier (FLC)
items.description: CIF-issued FLC with name tape sewn on rear drag handle
items.whyNeeded: Required fighting load carrier for all tactical operations during SUT.
items.selectionTips: Sew your name tape on the rear drag handle before arriving. CIF issue only.

items.name: Compass, Magnetic with Case
items.description: CIF-issued magnetic compass (2 required)
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is a primary skill evaluated throughout SUT. Two compasses required.
items.selectionTips: Know how to shoot an azimuth cold. Practice for months before arriving.

recommendedProducts.name: Cammenga 3H Tritium Lensatic Compass
recommendedProducts.reason: The Army-standard compass used by SF cadre. Tritium glow allows accurate azimuth shots in complete darkness — no batteries, no failure.

items.name: Sleep System (Inner + Outer Bag)
items.description: USGI modular sleep system — both inner and outer bags required
items.whyNeeded: Camp Mackall temperatures can drop below freezing. Hypothermia ends SF careers.
items.selectionTips: Inner bag rated to 30°F alone. Combined system rated to -10°F. Outer bag required 1 Oct – 30 Mar.

recommendedProducts.name: USGI Modular Sleep System (MSS)
recommendedProducts.reason: The exact system used at Camp Mackall. Proven across all climate phases of SFQC — the same setup SF candidates have used for decades.

items.name: Gore-Tex Bivy Cover
items.description: CIF-issued waterproof bivy cover for sleep system
items.whyNeeded: North Carolina weather is unpredictable. Bivy cover protects your sleep system from rain.
items.selectionTips: Ensure it is serviceable. Check seams before arriving.

items.name: Wet Weather Jacket & Trousers (Level VI)
items.description: CIF-issued Gore-Tex parka and trousers
items.whyNeeded: Mandatory for winter classes (1 Oct – 30 Mar). Required for all field operations.
items.selectionTips: PCU Level 5/6 combined may substitute during winter classes. Verify with cadre.

items.name: Helmet, ACH with OCP Cover
items.description: Advanced Combat Helmet with OCP cover, chinstrap, and 7 foam impact pads
items.whyNeeded: Required for all tactical evolutions and weapons ranges during SUT.
items.selectionTips: ACU-colored impact pads only: 1 crown, 2 trapezoid, 4 oval. Ensure chinstrap is serviceable.

items.name: Canteens (1QT x2 and 2QT x2)
items.description: CIF-issued 1-quart and 2-quart canteens with covers
items.whyNeeded: Hydration is survival. All canteens and covers must be CIF issue.
items.selectionTips: Plus canteen cups. CamelBak is additionally authorized.

recommendedProducts.name: CamelBak BFM 3L Hydration Pack
recommendedProducts.reason: CamelBak is additionally authorized for SUT. Hands-free 3L capacity keeps you hydrated through long patrol bases without breaking from your position.

items.name: Poncho (Camo/ACU/OCP) or CIF Small Tarp
items.description: CIF-issued camouflage poncho or small tarp (2 required)
items.whyNeeded: Primary rain protection and field shelter construction.
items.selectionTips: CIF issue only. Poncho liner (woobie) is a highly recommended optional addition.

items.name: OCP Blouse and Trouser Sets
items.description: 4 sets minimum. Standard issue only — no Patagonia or Crye uniforms
items.whyNeeded: Worn throughout all three weeks. Mark every item with rank, name, and US Army.
items.selectionTips: Uniform quantities include what is worn. Bring minimum 4 sets + what you wear on day 1.

items.name: Combat Boots (AR 670-1 Compliant)
items.description: 2 pairs minimum of hot weather or intermediate cold boots
items.whyNeeded: Foot health is your #1 asset at Camp Mackall. Blisters can end your SFQC.
items.selectionTips: Break in both pairs for months before arriving. Rotate pairs daily. Cold weather boots required 1 Oct – 30 Mar.

recommendedProducts.name: Danner Ft. Lewis 10" Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Full-grain leather built for Camp Mackall's varied terrain. The boot most chosen by SF candidates — durable enough for weeks of continuous field use.

items.name: Wool Socks (Green, Grey, Coyote, or Black)
items.description: 6 pairs minimum
items.whyNeeded: Mandatory AR 670-1 compliant wool socks. Moisture management prevents blisters and trench foot.
items.selectionTips: Darn Tough. Change every evolution if possible. Bring more than the minimum.

recommendedProducts.name: Darn Tough Boot Full Cushion Sock
recommendedProducts.reason: Lifetime guarantee and merino wool construction. The only sock proven to survive weeks of continuous field operations in North Carolina's variable climate.

items.name: 3-Day Army Assault MOLLE II Backpack
items.description: Military-issue assault pack
items.whyNeeded: Required secondary pack for lighter-load operations.
items.selectionTips: Must be military issue. OCP pattern.

items.name: IPFU Uniform (Complete)
items.description: Army PT uniform — 2 shorts, 2 shirts, long pants, jacket
items.whyNeeded: Required for PT formation and authorized PT time.
items.selectionTips: Complete set only. All items must be current ARMY-branded issue.

items.name: Cold Weather Underwear (Army Issue)
items.description: 2 sets minimum of Army-issue cold weather base layers
items.whyNeeded: Mandatory during winter classes (1 Oct – 30 Mar). Regulates core temperature during cold-weather operations.
items.selectionTips: Army issue only. No Under Armour or civilian compression shirts.

items.name: Leather Gloves
items.description: 2 pairs of AR 670-1 compliant leather gloves
items.whyNeeded: Required during cold operations and weapons handling.
items.selectionTips: Black or green only. Carry two pairs to account for the wet environment.

recommendedProducts.name: Military Mechanics Gloves (Leather)
recommendedProducts.reason: Durable AR 670-1 compliant leather palm gloves trusted by SF candidates at Camp Mackall.

items.name: Headlamp with Red Filter (2) + Spare Batteries
items.description: Two headlamps with red filter capability and spare batteries
items.whyNeeded: Night operations are constant during SUT. Red filter preserves night vision. Two required in case one fails.
items.selectionTips: AA-battery preferred for field resupply. No rechargeable-only models.

recommendedProducts.name: Princeton Tec Remix Pro Headlamp
recommendedProducts.reason: Trusted by SF cadre for its reliability. 300+ hour runtime on standard AA batteries — essential when there's no USB charging in the field.

items.name: Personal Hygiene Kit (6-Week Supply)
items.description: Razors (10+ non-electric), toothbrush/paste (2+), soap, shaving cream, chapstick
items.whyNeeded: Students cannot use the shopette during SUT. All hygiene supplies must last the full course.
items.selectionTips: Minimum 10 disposable razors. No aerosol cans. Antibacterial soap.

items.name: Running Shoes
items.description: 1 pair of serviceable running shoes for PT
items.whyNeeded: Required for PT formations and morning runs.
items.selectionTips: Something well broken-in. HOKA or Brooks for cushioning.

recommendedProducts.name: HOKA Clifton 9 Running Shoe
recommendedProducts.reason: Maximum cushioning for PT runs after long field operations. Protects stress fracture-prone feet during high-mileage SFQC phases.

items.name: Notebook with Pen and Pencil
items.description: Field-grade notebooks as required
items.whyNeeded: SUT is academically demanding. Recording TAC briefs, OPORD formats, and patrol orders is essential.
items.selectionTips: Rite in the Rain waterproof notebooks recommended. Bring multiple.

recommendedProducts.name: Rite in the Rain All-Weather Notebook
recommendedProducts.reason: Waterproof paper survives North Carolina rain during patrol bases. Essential for recording OPORD formats and TAC briefs in the field.

items.name: Foot Powder, Anti-Fungal
items.description: 1 bottle anti-fungal foot powder
items.whyNeeded: Camp Mackall's wet conditions create trench foot risk. Anti-fungal powder is listed as a mandatory item.
items.selectionTips: Apply daily, especially after water crossings.

items.name: Wristwatch (Non-GPS, Durable)
items.description: Durable mechanical or digital watch — no GPS or wireless data
items.whyNeeded: Required for timing patrol operations and coordinating actions on the objective.
items.selectionTips: No GPS, no Bluetooth, no smart capabilities. Casio G-Shock is the standard SF candidate choice.

items.name: Insect Repellent / Chigg-Away
items.description: 2 bottles, military or civilian (no aerosol cans)
items.whyNeeded: Camp Mackall is known for chiggers, ticks, and mosquitoes. Insect repellent is a listed mandatory item.
items.selectionTips: Pump spray or lotion only. No aerosol cans authorized.

items.name: Leatherman Multi-Tool (Highly Recommended)
items.description: Quality multi-tool for field repairs
items.selectionTips: Highly recommended by SUT cadre. Used constantly for weapons maintenance and field repairs.

recommendedProducts.name: Leatherman Wave+ Multi-Tool
recommendedProducts.reason: 25 tools in 8.5 oz. The go-to for SF candidates at Camp Mackall — weapons maintenance, field repairs, and first aid all covered.

items.name: Poncho Liner (Woobie) — Highly Recommended
items.description: Additional warmth layer and field versatility
items.selectionTips: Explicitly listed as 'highly recommended' in the official packing list. The most versatile piece of field kit in the Army.

items.name: Dump Pouch / Drop Pouch (Highly Recommended)
items.description: Magazine dump pouch for FLC/MOLLE
items.selectionTips: Highly recommended by cadre for magazine management during live-fire and blank-fire lanes.

items.name: Knife (Max 7" Blade, Folding/Pocket Preferred)
items.description: Field knife within authorized dimensions
items.selectionTips: Folding or pocket style preferred. Max blade length 7 inches. Keep it sharp.

items.name: Ranger Handbook (April 2017 Edition)
items.description: SH 21-76 or SUT Handbook
items.selectionTips: Available for issue by cadre. Having your own tabbed copy saves time when your mind is foggy from lack of sleep.

recommendedProducts.name: US Army Ranger Handbook SH 21-76
recommendedProducts.reason: The definitive reference for OPORD formats, battle drills, and patrol base operations. Tab the most-referenced sections before arrival.

items.name: This is a test
items.description: STest 123 456 234 Hello jimmy I really think this is cool.
items.selectionTips: Hello this is a normal test. Nothing to see here 123.

recommendedProducts.name: This is a test of the store
recommendedProducts.reason: I dont know what this code does.

items.name: This is a test part 2
items.description: STest 123 456 234 Hello jimmy I really think this is cool.
items.selectionTips: Hello this is a normal test. Nothing to see here 123.

recommendedProducts.name: This is a test of the store
recommendedProducts.reason: I dont know what this code does.

items.name: Unauthorized Electronics
items.description: MP3 players, cameras, voice recorders, media storage devices, GPS devices
items.notes: Any unauthorized electronic device is a violation of the Student Honor Code and results in immediate RELIEF from SUT.

items.name: Civilian GPS Units
items.description: Any civilian GPS device of any kind
items.notes: Students are issued the DAGR (Defense Advanced GPS Receiver) by SUT cadre. No civilian GPS of any kind allowed.

items.name: Patagonia or Crye Uniforms
items.description: Commercially procured combat shirts or non-standard uniforms
items.notes: Standard issue OCP only. No Patagonia, Crye, or 'combat shirts' authorized. Must be CIF-issued items.

items.name: Compression T-Shirts
items.description: Under Armour or similar compression base layer shirts
items.notes: Specifically prohibited in SUT notes. Army-issue T-shirts and cold weather underwear only.
